introSection: Crazy Cat is a steel roller coaster that travelled with Wade Shows, Inc. in the USA. It was owned by Wade Shows from 1999 or earlier. It was replaced by the Super Cyclone.

sections.title: History
sections.content: The coaster operated at the Florida State Fair in February 2014 and the Seminole County Fair in 2015.

sections.title: Name controversy
sections.content: The ride was originally named Zyklon, however the name was changed after a complaint about its similarity to Zyklon-B, the trade name for the gas used to poison people at concentration camps during the Holocaust.

sections.title: External links
sections.content: * Official webpage for Crazy Cat Coaster

fullText:
Crazy Cat is a steel roller coaster that travelled with Wade Shows, Inc. in the USA. It was owned by Wade Shows from 1999 or earlier. It was replaced by the Super Cyclone.

The coaster operated at the Florida State Fair in February 2014 and the Seminole County Fair in 2015.

The ride was originally named Zyklon, however the name was changed after a complaint about its similarity to Zyklon-B, the trade name for the gas used to poison people at concentration camps during the Holocaust.


 * Official webpage for Crazy Cat Coaster
